narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students just like you. Your Year 3 decile of 3 stood out as the biggest chapter in this story, signaling to them that your performance dipped noticeably in the later stretch of law school, which has been the strongest predictor of bar struggles for many others. That was compounded by your LSAT score of 32, which, while solid for some paths, pulled the narrative firmly toward higher risks of not passing, as it's often a key benchmark for the intense reasoning demands ahead. Your Year 1 decile of 4 added to this picture, suggesting an early pattern of being in the middle of the pack rather than rising to the top where bar success stories tend to cluster. Coming from a middle-income family influenced things too, as the model has learned that this bracket sometimes correlates with added pressures that tip the scales toward failure for applicants with your other traits. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.2 fit into this storyline as another moderate signal, not quite lifting you above the risks the earlier factors painted. Even your background as a white female played a subtle role in this direction, based on historical trends the team weighs carefully. The one bright note was your choice to go full-time, which nudged things slightly toward success by showing commitment without work distractions. Together, these elements added up to our model seeing about a 40% chance of bar failure—enough risk that we had to make the tough call to reject your application, not because we doubt your potential, but to protect the program's outcomes and your future steps. We truly see your strengths and the effort behind that LSAT and GPA, and this isn't the end of your story—perhaps targeting schools with stronger bar support or bolstering those later deciles could rewrite it next time. Keep pushing; you've got the foundation to turn this around.
